summary: Handled a user-provided GitHub personal access token as sensitive material. Did not use, echo, persist, or configure the exposed token. Advised revoking it and using GitHub CLI browser auth or a newly generated fine-grained token with narrow repository scope and expiration.
decisions: Treat the pasted GitHub token as compromised because it appeared in chat.
Do not use or store raw tokens in shell commands, git config, repo files, memory, or task traces.
Recommend GitHub CLI browser login as the safest path for release/API automation.
next_steps: Revoke/delete the exposed token in GitHub settings.
Generate a new fine-grained token only if browser login is unavailable; scope it to clockmansy/dinobrain and clockmansy/dinobrain-data with minimal permissions and an expiration date.
Install GitHub CLI and authenticate with gh auth login --web before release asset automation.
